title: No Start Diagnostic PID Bundle
description: Captures the critical live data and sensor readings needed to diagnose a no-start condition. Covers: cranks-but-won't-start vs won't-crank. All via ELM327.
physical_solution: Use PID bundle to identify which system is at fault: fuel (pressure/crank signal), spark (coil/crank), air (throttle), or security (immo). Isolate before replacing parts.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Full — all PIDs in this bundle are Mode $01 standard. Key-on readings available without engine running.
preconditions: Key ON (KOEO) first, then KOEC (Key On Engine Cranking) readings
Note: if immobilizer active, security light will flash during crank

procedure_steps.description: BATTERY/VOLTAGE: Mode $01 PID $42 (control module voltage). KOEO: should be 12.4V+. During cranking: should not drop below 9.5V. Below 9.5V cranking = weak battery or starter draw. Fix charging system before diagnosing no-start.

CRANK SIGNAL: Mode $01 PID $0C (RPM). During cranking: should show 150-300 RPM. If PID shows 0 RPM while starter is engaged = no crank signal to ECU (CKP sensor fault, timing belt/chain broken, or ECU no-power).

COOLANT TEMP: Mode $01 PID $05. Cold start: should show actual ambient temp (proves sensor reading correctly). If reading -40°C or 150°C+ = sensor fault causing incorrect fueling strategy. -40°C = sensor open circuit.

THROTTLE POSITION: Mode $01 PID $11. KOEO with foot off pedal: should be 0-15%. If 100% = 'clear flood mode' — ECU thinks pedal is floored, cuts fuel. Verify actual pedal position. If mismatch = TPS or APP sensor fault.

FUEL PRESSURE (if available): Mode $01 PID $0A. KOEO (before cranking): fuel pump should prime — pressure should be 40-60 psi within 2 seconds of key-on. If pressure stays at 0 = pump not priming (relay, fuse, pump failure).

READ ALL DTCs: $19 02 FF to ECM. Note any P0335/P0336 (CKP), P0340/P0341 (CMP), P0230 (fuel pump), U-codes (network). No codes + cranks-no-start often = spark or fuel (not ECU-detectable without running).

SECURITY CHECK: Does security light flash during crank? If yes = immobilizer active. Key programming issue or transponder fault. See immobilizer_key_programming.json. If no security indicator = immo not the cause.

SPARK TEST: Remove one coil, attach test spark plug, ground spark plug body to engine block, crank — should see bright blue spark. Orange/yellow weak spark = coil fault. No spark = no ignition trigger (CKP or ignition module).

After identifying and fixing root cause: verify PID $0C shows 150-300 RPM cranking, $42 voltage stays above 9.5V, $05 reads realistic temp, $11 throttle near 0%, fuel pressure primes on key-on. Engine should start.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Common no-start causes: crankshaft position sensor (P0335), fuel pump relay (check IGSW relay), immobilizer (security light pattern). D-4ST engines: check both LP and HP fuel systems.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Common: main relay failure (older models), CKP sensor. PGM-FI relay on older Accord/CR-V is notorious. Check under dash relay box. Mode $01 PID $0C during crank shows 0 RPM if CKP failed.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Common: IPDM-ER (Intelligent Power Distribution Module) fuel pump relay failure. CKP sensor on VQ engines. CVT no-start: some CVTs won't allow start if P17F0 is active.
oem_notes.Ford: Common: EcoBoost fuel pump low pressure (no-start after hot soak). PATS immo fault (most common after battery disconnect). Mode $01 PID $42 during crank — Ford drops to 8V with failing battery.
oem_notes.GM: Common: passlock/passkey immo (security light on = 10-minute learn procedure). Crankshaft position sensor on V8s (P0336). Check SECM (Security Control Module) DTC alongside engine DTCs.
oem_notes.BMW: Common: CAS module immo sync after battery disconnect. HP fuel pump no-start. DME power supply relay. Mode $01 RPM shows 0 if CKP failed during crank.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: Common: EVAP/leak detection pump codes interfering with start. Immobilizer PIN issue after cluster replacement. EA888: HPFP failure causes crank-no-start with fuel system lean codes.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Common: TIPM (Totally Integrated Power Module) fuel pump relay failure on 2007-2016 Jeep/Chrysler — extremely common. Security light flash = SKIM immo active. TIPM relay clicking = confirm fuel pump priming.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Common: crankshaft position sensor failure. Theta II: engine seizure causes no-start (check oil pressure history). Smart key battery dead (fob battery, not car battery). Mode $01 PID $0C shows 0 RPM if CKP failed.
